content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 03. Prompt Pack
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
Document section: 03. Prompt Pack / 16. Что обычно ломает memory prompts .. 17. Что я бы включил в первый релиз
Document lines: 1035-1064

## 16. Что обычно ломает memory prompts

- один prompt на все стадии сразу
- отсутствие existing memory в reconcile prompt
- отсутствие cardinality/conflict rules
- отсутствие scope и time semantics
- слишком широкий контекст
- отсутствие явного `noop`
- разрешение модели писать свободный prose вместо схемы
- over-generation notes

## 17. Что я бы включил в первый релиз

Если резать до полезного минимума, то сначала я бы взял:

1. `MemoryRouter`
2. `WriteTimeRetrievalPlanner`
3. `ClaimExtractor`
4. `ClaimReconciler`
5. `TaskUpdater`
6. `ProfileUpdater`
7. `ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner`
8. `AnswerComposer`

А затем уже добавлял:

9. `NoteConstructor`
10. `NoteReconciler`
11. `NoteConsolidator`
12. `MemoryRepairPlanner`

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=LESSON title=Common failure modes for memory prompts summary=Sections 16 lists recurring causes of memory prompt failures: monolithic multi-stage prompting, missing existing-memory context in reconcile, no cardinality/conflict rules, no scope/time semantics, overly broad context, missing explicit noop path, allowing free prose instead of strict schema, and over-generation of notes. scope=Applies to section "03. Prompt Pack / 16. Что обычно ломает memory prompts" in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1> keywords=memory prompts,failure modes,noop,scope semantics,over-generation notes tags=prompt_design,memory_hygiene,lessons evidence=## 16. Что обычно ломает memory prompts

Prompt Pack / 8. Profile Updater v3" in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md. keywords=Profile Updater v3,profile_json,profile_text,projection,when to call tags=doc_digest,memory_pipeline,profile_updater_v3 useCount=0

Prompt Pack / 9. Task Updater v3" in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md. keywords=Task Updater v3,insert update close cancel noop,commitments,deadlines,due_at tags=doc_digest,memory_pipeline,task_updater_v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note Consolidator v2 background consolidation policy and output contract summary=In 03. Prompt Pack section 10, Note Consolidator v2 runs in background after debounce/batch windows to review mature notes and selectively promote strong signals into durable memory (claims/tasks/episodes) while managing note lifecycle via note_actions. scope=Applies to section "03. Prompt Pack / 10. Note Consolidator v2" in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md. keywords=Note Consolidator v2,mature notes,durable structured memory,note_actions tags=doc_digest,memory_lifecycle,consolidation useCount=0

Read-Time Retrieval Planner v3" in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=Read-Time Retrieval Planner v3,need_memory,answer_mode,retrieval_budget,require_evidence_fallback tags=prompt_pack,retrieval_planning,json_contract useCount=0

Prompt Pack summary=Section 0 defines shared input and semantic guardrails for all memory prompts: pass core runtime context, enforce strict JSON validation/versioning, prefer noop over noisy memory, prioritize explicit over inferred statements, normalize relative time to absolute, keep one reusable unit per memory item, and constrain generalization via scope_json. scope=Applies to section "0. Общие правила для всех memory prompts" in 03. Prompt Pack (agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md). keywords=memory prompts,general rules,scope_json,explicit vs inference,JSON validation tags=prompt_pack,memory_policy,doc_section useCount=0

Prompt Pack section 6, ClaimExtractor v3 is run when router mode is `direct_structured_write` or `mixed` (and optionally by background consolidator) to extract atomic facts into a strict JSON claim schema with one predicate application per claim and scoped, high-precision output. scope=Applies to 03. Prompt Pack / 6. Claim Extractor v3 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=ClaimExtractor v3,direct_structured_write,mixed,atomic facts,claim schema tags=prompt_pack,claim_extraction,workflow useCount=0

Memory Router v3" in 03. Prompt Pack (agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md). keywords=Memory Router v3,decision policy,write mode,noop,direct_structured_write tags=router_v3,memory_pipeline,decision_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<10> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 Prompt Pack favors narrow, testable prompts over one monolithic lifecycle prompt summary=In 03. Prompt Pack, the starter prompt pack for `v3` is intentionally designed not to put the entire memory lifecycle into one giant prompt; instead, each step should remain narrow and verifiable. scope=Design direction stated in section "03. Prompt Pack" of agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md for v3 prompt structuring. keywords=v3 prompt pack,memory lifecycle,narrow steps,testable prompts tags=prompt_design,architecture_direction,v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<11> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory Repair Planner v1 conservative repair strategy and action schema summary=In section 11, Memory Repair Planner v1 is a background maintenance component for suspicious clusters or post prompt/model changes, producing conservative repair_actions (e.g., merge_duplicates, supersede_item, archive_item, downgrade_retrieval_state, refresh_profile) to improve memory hygiene while preserving history. scope=Applies to section "03. Prompt Pack / 11. Memory Repair Planner v1" in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md. keywords=Memory Repair Planner v1,repair_actions,suspicious clusters,conservative tags=doc_digest,memory_hygiene,repair_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<12> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Minimum validation layer defines deterministic guards outside model prompts summary=Section 15 establishes a mandatory deterministic validation baseline outside the model: schema validation, catalog predicate checks, XOR of object fields, confidence/range and time consistency checks, normalized_text non-empty, evidence requirement for active claims, scope_json shape checks, and retrieval budget cap enforcement. scope=Applies to section "03. Prompt Pack / 15. Minimum validation layer outside the model" in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=deterministic guards,validation layer,schema validation,retrieval budget caps tags=doc_digest,validation,safety useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<13> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=FastSlowEscalationPlanner v1 decides fast-to-slow escalation after draft answer summary=In 03. Prompt Pack section 14, FastSlowEscalationPlanner v1 is an optional post-draft fast-path step that decides whether to escalate to slow path and returns a structured handoff (user_goal, believed facts, open questions, uncertainty flags, required outputs). Escalation is guided by ambiguity/conflict/rationale/verification risk, while well-grounded answers should stay on fast path. scope=Applies to section "03. Prompt Pack / 14. Optional FastSlow Escalation Planner v1" in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=FastSlowEscalationPlanner v1,fast path,slow path,handoff schema tags=doc_digest,escalation_policy,routing useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
